content.full_text:
July 4, 1990

Mary Ann Kvasager  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95814-0814

Dear Mary Ann:

This will confirm our telephone conversation on August 3, 1990. I inquired regarding the discount exception under the definition of contribution contained in Government Code Section 82015. Specifically, I asked if a hotel were to grant discounts on its hotel rooms to a preexisting preferred list of best customers of the hotel, which list includes a current candidate for office, would the amount of the discount constitute a contribution to the candidate?

You checked with Carla Wardlow and Jeanne Pritchard and advised me that the list of preferred hotel customers, while being a smaller universe of the public generally, would still be considered the public generally for purposes of the statute. Therefore, no contribution of the discounted amount would be involved by providing a candidate, who is on the hotel's pre-existing best customer list, with a discounted room price.

Thank you very much for this advice.

Very truly yours,

[handwritten signature] Peter A. Bagatelos

Peter A. Bagatelos


- "THE INTERNATIONAL BUILDING"
- "60 CENTURY AVENUE"
- "SUITE 1200"
- "SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94108"
- "BAGATELOS & FADEM"
- "BETH A. BAGATELOS"
- "BARRY FADEM"

The contact information includes:
- PHONE: (415) 385-4100
- FAX: (415) 385-4000

There is also a mention of "Law Offices of" at the top, indicating this might be related to a law firm.
